title: Phylon v0: Designing a Bit-Exact Artificial Life Engine in Rust
summary: Why bit-exact reproducibility is non-negotiable for scientific simulation, and how Phylon enforces it across platforms using fixed-timestep updates and a 30-crate DAG workspace.

title: Why ECS Outperforms OOP for Large-Scale Agent Simulation
summary: A deep dive into why Data-Oriented Design and Entity-Component-System architecture consistently outperforms object-oriented approaches when simulating 100,000+ autonomous agents in real time.

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks: What They Are and Why They Matter for Biology
summary: An accessible introduction to PINNs for researchers coming from a biology or ecology background. Covers the core idea of embedding differential equations as loss terms and why this is powerful for modeling living systems.
